title: System Ownership

content.question: What dependency in your life is quietly making you rentable, not free?
content.mental_model: If you don't own it, you depend on it.
sop: List your top 5 dependencies: platforms, landlords, single clients, employers, suppliers.
Rate your Ownership and Dependency for each, 1-10 (see calculation, page 4), how much you control it versus how much it controls you.
Pick the dependency with the worst ratio and identify one owned alternative (your own list, your own space, a second client).
Build that owned alternative in parallel, don't wait until the dependency fails.
Reduce reliance on the weakest dependency by a measurable amount this quarter.
questions: What dependency in your life is quietly making you rentable, not free?
If the platform, landlord, or client you depend on most changed the terms tomorrow, how much control would you actually lose?
What do you currently rent (attention, audience, space, income source) that you could instead own?
content.equation_example: Ownership (rate 1-10: how much you control it) divided by Dependency (rate 1-10: how much you rely on someone else for it) equals Control score. Ownership 8 divided by Dependency 6 equals 1.3. Above 1 means you have more control than exposure; below 1 means you're more exposed than you are in control.
content.closing_statement: Own what you can't afford to lose access to.

caption:
Principle Nº 187: System Ownership

Control = ownership.

Ask yourself: What dependency in your life is quietly making you rentable, not free?
